openmeter/billing/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL implementation of billing.Adapter — the single persistence boundary for all billing domain entities (profiles, customer overrides, invoices, invoice lines, split line groups, sequences, locks, schema migration). Primary constraint: every method must compose with caller-supplied ctx transactions.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method body
patterns.description: Wrap write/read-consistent methods in entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so a.db rebinds to the ctx-carried tx via WithTx; nesting is safe.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (*billing.CustomerOverride, error) { row, err := tx.db.BillingCustomerOverride.Create()....Save(ctx); return mapCustomerOverrideFromDB(row) })

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions at file top
patterns.description: Each file implementing a sub-interface declares var _ billing.XxxAdapter = (*adapter)(nil) to catch missing methods at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ billing.GatheringInvoiceAdapter = (*adapter)(nil)

patterns.name: workflowConfigWithTaxCode eager-load option
patterns.description: Every BillingProfile/WorkflowConfig query that will be mapped must pass WithWorkflowConfig(workflowConfigWithTaxCode); omitting it yields silent nil TaxCode in BackfillTaxConfig.
patterns.example: tx.db.BillingProfile.Query().Where(...).WithWorkflowConfig(workflowConfigWithTaxCode).First(ctx)

patterns.name: Manually attach edges after Save()
patterns.description: Ent never populates edges on mutation results; after Save with a TaxCodeID, fetch and assign the edge before returning.
patterns.example: if saved.TaxCodeID != nil { tc, _ := a.db.TaxCode.Get(ctx, *saved.TaxCodeID); saved.Edges.TaxCode = tc }

patterns.name: entitydiff-based upsert for line hierarchies
patterns.description: Update invoice lines via diffInvoiceLines/diffGatheringInvoiceLines (Create/Update/Delete sets) then bulk CREATE ON CONFLICT; never item-by-item.
patterns.example: lineDiffs, _ := diffInvoiceLines(input.Lines); tx.upsertFeeLineConfig(ctx, lineDiffs.DetailedLine)

patterns.name: DBState snapshot after read
patterns.description: After mapping a line from DB call line.SaveDBSnapshot(); the diff engine uses DBState, so omitting makes every field look changed on next write.
patterns.example: if err := line.SaveDBSnapshot(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving DB snapshot [id=%s]: %w", line.GetID(), err) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete with DeletedAt
patterns.description: Billing entities soft-delete via SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()); queries must add Where(...DeletedAtIsNil()) unless IncludeDeleted. Sequences and lock rows are never soft-deleted.
patterns.example: tx.db.BillingInvoice.Update().Where(billinginvoice.ID(id)).SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).Save(ctx)

key_file_guides.role: Config, adapter struct (db *entdb.Client, logger), New, and the Tx/WithTx/Self triad required by entutils.TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tx() uses HijackTx — do not replace with db.BeginTx as it breaks the transaction driver contract.

key_file_guides.role: Implements UpsertInvoiceLines — multi-table upsert across BillingInvoiceLine, flat-fee/usage-based configs, discounts, schema-level-2 detailed lines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SchemaLevel gates which detail table is written (schema 1 FlatFeeLine vs schema 2 DetailedLine); mixing corrupts reads.

key_file_guides.role: Pure diffing logic (no DB) for line hierarchies; produces invoiceLineDiff with separate entitydiff.Diff sets.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Equality uses StandardLineBase.Equal / UsageBased.Equal — new fields require updating Equal or diffs are missed.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD/diff/upsert for gathering lines (usage-based) plus expandSplitLineHierarchy for progressive billing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Gathering lines hard-delete via HardDeleteGatheringInvoiceLines — must atomically delete associated UsageBasedLineConfig rows.

key_file_guides.role: Lazy per-customer invoice schema migration (level 1→2) via raw SQL proc om_func_migrate_customer_invoices_to_schema_level_2.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call migrateCustomerInvoices outside a LockCustomerForUpdate context.

key_file_guides.role: Profile/WorkflowConfig CRUD; manually fetches TaxCode edge after Save.
key_file_guides.watch_for: workflowConfigWithTaxCode must be used on every mapped BillingProfile query.

key_file_guides.role: Sequence allocation with FOR UPDATE pessimistic locking, upsert-then-reselect for first-use race.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NextSequenceNumber must run inside a transaction (uses ForUpdate).

key_file_guides.role: Pure DB→domain mapping; backfillTaxConfigReferences handles legacy stripe-code/TaxCodeID precedence.
key_file_guides.watch_for: mapStandardInvoiceDetailedLineFromDB is schema-1; mapStandardInvoiceDetailedLineV2FromDB is schema-2 — caller selects via schemaLevelByInvoiceID.

anti_patterns: Using a.db directly inside a helper without entutils.TransactingRepo — ignores the ctx-carried tx and produces partial writes.
Querying BillingProfile/WorkflowConfig without WithWorkflowConfig(workflowConfigWithTaxCode) — silent nil TaxCode.
Returning a Save() result without manually attaching the TaxCode edge — mappers dereference nil.
Bypassing the entitydiff pipeline to write lines one-by-one — misses AffectedLineIDs/UpdatedAt propagation.
Calling migrateCustomerInvoices outside a LockCustomerForUpdate context.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo at each adapter method boundary, not once in the service layer.
decisions.rationale: Methods compose (GetCustomerOverride inside UpdateCustomerOverride); TransactingRepo safely nests by reusing the ctx tx, keeping service free of tx management.

decisions.decision: Entity-diff + bulk-upsert pipeline for invoice lines instead of load-compare-update.
decisions.rationale: Line sets can be large; diffing then bulk CREATE ON CONFLICT minimises round trips and propagates UpdatedAt only to changed lines.

decisions.decision: Schema migration is lazy under LockCustomerForUpdate, not a global offline job.
decisions.rationale: A global migration needs a long exclusive lock; per-customer lazy migration amortises cost without blocking unrelated customers.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method that reads/writes inside a transaction

openmeter/billing/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler package for billing — translates between api.* types and billing.* service inputs/outputs, registers the billing errorEncoder, and exposes the composite Handler interface consumed by openmeter/server/router. Each endpoint is a method on *handler returning an httptransport handler, never ServeHTTP.

patterns.name: Handler method returns typed httptransport handler
patterns.description: Each operation is a method on *handler returning httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] or HandlerWithArgs; it only constructs the handler, never calls ServeHTTP.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) GetProfile() GetProfileHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decode, operate, encode, opts...) }

patterns.name: Type alias triple per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares Request, Response, Params (and a Handler) type aliases at the top.
patterns.example: type ( GetProfileRequest = billing.GetProfileInput; GetProfileResponse = api.BillingProfile; GetProfileParams struct { ID string }; GetProfileHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...] )

patterns.name: resolveNamespace first in every decoder
patterns.description: Every decode calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first; returns InternalServerError if missing. Namespace is never inlined.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

patterns.name: errorEncoder() appended to every handler
patterns.description: errors.go's errorEncoder() maps billing.NotFoundError→404, ValidationError→400, UpdateAfterDeleteError→409; appended via httptransport.AppendOptions on every handler.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("ListProfiles"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()))

patterns.name: Deprecated dual-field parsing centralized
patterns.description: Endpoints accepting both rateCard and deprecated price/featureKey/taxConfig use mapAndValidateInvoiceLineRateCardDeprecatedFields from deprecations.go.
patterns.example: rateCardParsed, err := mapAndValidateInvoiceLineRateCardDeprecatedFields(invoiceLineRateCardItems{ RateCard: line.RateCard, Price: line.Price })

patterns.name: Pagination defaults from defaults.go
patterns.description: List handlers use DefaultPageSize=100/DefaultPageNumber=1 via lo.FromPtrOr or defaultx.WithDefault, never hard-coded inline.
patterns.example: Page: pagination.Page{ PageSize: defaultx.WithDefault(params.PageSize, DefaultPageSize), PageNumber: defaultx.WithDefault(params.Page, DefaultPageNumber) }

key_file_guides.role: Composite Handler interface (ProfileHandler + InvoiceLineHandler + InvoiceHandler + CustomerOverrideHandler), handler struct, New().
key_file_guides.watch_for: New interface methods must be implemented on *handler and registered in router.Config or you get a compile error or 404.

key_file_guides.role: Single errorEncoder() mapping billing domain errors to HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New billing error types must be added here; omitting them yields 500 instead of 4xx.

key_file_guides.role: Backward-compat parsing of legacy price/featureKey/taxConfig alongside rateCard.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add deprecated bridge logic here; never inline dual-field compatibility in endpoint decoders.
ProgressInvoice uses a ProgressAction discriminator — add new actions to both InvoiceProgressActions and invoiceProgressOperationNames.
MapProfileToApi has two paths (p.Apps vs p.AppReferences) — keep both in sync when adding profile fields.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level pagination/display defaults for list handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Reference via lo.FromPtrOr/defaultx.WithDefault — never hard-code page size inline.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Service methods from a decode function — decode only parses; operate calls the service.
Returning ValidationError/NotFoundError without errorEncoder() — yields 500 instead of 4xx.
Adding Handler interface methods without implementing on *handler and updating handler.go's interface.
Inlining deprecated-field compatibility logic outside deprecations.go.
Using context.Background() instead of the caller's ctx in a handler closure.

decisions.decision: Two-closure decode+operate httptransport pattern rather than a single ServeHTTP.
decisions.rationale: Separates namespace/input parsing from business logic and lets the framework handle error encoding and OTel tracing uniformly.

decisions.decision: errorEncoder() defined once and appended to every handler.
decisions.rationale: Ensures consistent HTTP status codes for billing errors without repeating type-switch logic per endpoint.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new billing HTTP endpoint

openmeter/billing/lineengine.purpose: Implements billing.LineEngine for the standard invoice line type — quantity snapshotting, split-line-group creation, period truncation, and per-line rating via rating.Service. Registered as LineEngineTypeInvoice in the billing.Service engine registry via app/common/charges.go.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() + New()
patterns.description: Engine constructed via Config.Validate() + New(Config) returning (*Engine, error); all deps injected through Config, never construct Engine directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Engine, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &Engine{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Narrow SplitLineGroupAdapter interface
patterns.description: splitlinegroup.go defines SplitLineGroupAdapter with only CreateSplitLineGroup/GetSplitLineGroupHeaders; never import full billing.Adapter or ent/db (prevents cycles).
patterns.example: type SplitLineGroupAdapter interface { CreateSplitLineGroup(ctx, billing.CreateSplitLineGroupAdapterInput) (billing.SplitLineGroup, error); GetSplitLineGroupHeaders(...) }

patterns.name: QuantitySnapshotter interface for dependency inversion
patterns.description: stdinvoice.go defines QuantitySnapshotter; concrete impl is billingservice.Service. Engine never calls the service directly.
patterns.example: type QuantitySnapshotter interface { SnapshotLineQuantities(ctx context.Context, invoice billing.StandardInvoice, lines billing.StandardLines) error }

patterns.name: billing.ValidationIssue for snapshot errors
patterns.description: When SnapshotLineQuantities returns ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState, convert to billing.ValidationIssue (Critical) so callers can type-assert.
patterns.example: if _, ok := lo.ErrorsAs[*billing.ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState](err); ok { return nil, billing.ValidationIssue{Severity: billing.ValidationIssueSeverityCritical, ...} }

patterns.name: isPeriodEmptyConsideringTruncations
patterns.description: Usage-based lines check emptiness after truncation to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration; flat-fee lines always return false.
patterns.example: return line.GetServicePeriod().Truncate(streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration).IsEmpty(), nil

key_file_guides.role: Implements billing.LineEngine: GetLineEngineType, OnCollectionCompleted, OnStandardInvoiceCreated, OnInvoiceIssued, OnPaymentAuthorized/Settled. OnCollectionCompleted is the primary snapshot hook.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OnCollectionCompleted short-circuits if QuantitySnapshotedAt is set at/after default collection time — add no snapshot logic after this guard.

key_file_guides.role: SplitGatheringLine splits a gathering line at a time, creates a SplitLineGroup, produces pre/post pairs; ResolveSplitLineGroupHeaders batch-fetches metadata.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChildUniqueReferenceID is set nil on split lines — the SplitLineGroup owns this reference; never copy from source.

key_file_guides.role: BuildStandardInvoiceLines converts gathering→standard lines, snapshots, runs CalculateLines (ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines per line).
key_file_guides.watch_for: CalculateLines requires non-empty lines; invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines runs after each GenerateDetailedLines call.

anti_patterns: Importing billing.Adapter or ent/db directly — use the narrow SplitLineGroupAdapter interface.
Calling SnapshotLineQuantities outside OnCollectionCompleted or BuildStandardInvoiceLines — snapshotting is lifecycle-gated.
Skipping ResolveSplitLineGroupHeaders before CalculateLines on lines with split groups — calculations depend on SplitLineHierarchy.
Returning plain errors from snapshot paths that should be billing.ValidationIssue.
Copying ChildUniqueReferenceID from source gathering line to split child lines.

decisions.decision: Engine registered at Service construction via Service.RegisterLineEngine, not hardcoded.
decisions.rationale: Lets charges sub-packages register their own engines without modifying the base service (LineEngine plugin registry pattern).

decisions.decision: SplitLineGroupAdapter and QuantitySnapshotter are narrow interfaces, not the full billing.Service.
decisions.rationale: lineengine is imported by billingservice (the QuantitySnapshotter impl); using the full interface would create a cycle.

code_examples.scenario: A LineEngine hook converting snapshot errors to ValidationIssue

openmeter/billing/testutils.purpose: Test utilities for the billing domain — provides NoopLineEngine, a billing.LineEngine stub for embedding in line-engine fakes. Intentionally does not implement billing.LineCalculator so test doubles selectively override only the hooks they need.

patterns.name: NoopLineEngine for test doubles
patterns.description: Pass-through billing.LineEngine (returns inputs unchanged, IsLineBillableAsOf returns true); embed and override only relevant methods.
patterns.example: type MyTestEngine struct { testutils.NoopLineEngine }; func (e *MyTestEngine) OnCollectionCompleted(ctx, input) (billing.StandardLines, error) { /* custom */ }

patterns.name: EngineType field must be set
patterns.description: GetLineEngineType() panics if EngineType is empty; always initialise.
patterns.example: engine := testutils.NoopLineEngine{EngineType: billing.LineEngineTypeInvoice}

key_file_guides.role: Defines NoopLineEngine — implements billing.LineEngine but not billing.LineCalculator; all hooks pass-through.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not run BuildStandardInvoiceLines in production via this type — it contains test-only stub logic (ToStandardLines) that bypasses snapshotting.

anti_patterns: Using NoopLineEngine in production wiring — it skips quantity snapshotting and rating.
Importing app/common from testutils — build deps from underlying constructors to avoid import cycles.
Adding production utility/domain code here — testutils must only contain test helpers.

decisions.decision: NoopLineEngine intentionally does not implement billing.LineCalculator.
decisions.rationale: Separates the minimum LineEngine registration interface from the optional calculator extension; doubles implement only what they need.

code_examples.scenario: Embedding NoopLineEngine to override one hook in a test
